warnings: sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose: Fukui reactivity analysis of furan computed at DFT tier 'standard' (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, PySCF RKS, exact four-center integrals — no density fitting), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Three single-point SCFs were run: neutral (charge 0, mult 1), cation (charge +1, mult 2), and anion (charge −1, mult 2). Geometry taken from the user-supplied .xyz file (not re-optimized). Partial charges via Mulliken (PySCF). All three SCFs converged; integrity.status = 'ok', trustworthy = true. Charge conservation: sum(f+) = 1.0000, sum(f-) = 1.0000. No engine warnings. The most electrophilic site is atom 5 (C, α-carbon) with f+ = 1.9242931; the most nucleophilic site is also atom 5 with f- = 0.16164. Note: negative Fukui f+ values on O and H atoms are a known Mulliken artifact with diffuse basis sets like def2-TZVP; the carbon-site trends are chemically consistent with furan's known preference for electrophilic aromatic substitution at the α-positions.
